note_data.title: Oscillations
keyPoints: One radian is defined as the angle in the sector of a circle when the arc length of that sector is equal to the radius of the circle
Angular displacement (θ) is the angle turned through by an object in any given direction in radians
Angular speed (ω) can be found using ω = v/r = 2π/T = 2πf, where v is linear speed, r is radius, T is time period, and f is frequency
An object moving in a circular path at constant speed has constantly changing velocity due to changing direction, therefore it must be accelerating
Centripetal acceleration always acts perpendicular to the motion of the object towards the centre of the circle
Centripetal acceleration can be calculated using a = v²/r = ω²r
Centripetal force is derived from Newton's second law: F = mv²/r = mω²r
Linear speed v can be calculated as the product of angular speed and radius: v = ωr

definitions.definition: The angle in the sector of a circle when the arc length of that sector is equal to the radius of the circle
The angle turned through by an object in any given direction in radians
The angle an object moves through per unit time
The acceleration of an object moving in a circular path, always acting perpendicular to motion towards the centre of the circle
The force that always acts perpendicular to the motion of an object moving in a circle, directed towards the centre of the circle

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State what happens in logical sequence without explanation
Draw clearly labelled axes with correct shape and key features marked

modelAnswer.question: A mass attached to a spring oscillates with SHM. Explain why the kinetic energy is maximum when the mass passes through the equilibrium position. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: At equilibrium position, displacement is zero so potential energy is zero. By conservation of energy, when potential energy is minimum, kinetic energy is maximum. Velocity is maximum at equilibrium position because acceleration changes direction at this point.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: At equilibrium, displacement = 0 so PE = 0
Mark 2: Conservation of energy means when PE minimum, KE maximum
Mark 3: Velocity maximum at equilibrium position

commonMistakes: Forgetting that velocity changes in circular motion even at constant speed due to changing direction
Confusing radians and degrees - remember to multiply by π/180 to convert degrees to radians
Thinking centripetal force acts outward instead of toward the center of the circle
Not recognizing that centripetal acceleration occurs even when speed is constant
examinerTips: Remember that a full circle equals 2π radians, which helps with conversions and calculations
Always check that centripetal force and acceleration are directed toward the center of the circular path
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Displacement-time graph showing sinusoidal curve with amplitude A and period T marked, plus velocity-time and acceleration-time graphs showing π/2 phase differences
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to calculate the period of a pendulum and explain how amplitude affects the motion
O/N 2022 P1 Q15 (2 marks) — multiple choice question on identifying simple harmonic motion from displacement-time graphs
M/J 2022 P2 Q3(c) (4 marks) — candidates had to describe energy changes during one complete oscillation of a mass-spring system
O/N 2021 P2 Q5 (8 marks) — comprehensive question on pendulum motion including calculations of frequency and gravitational field strength
M/J 2021 P1 Q18 (2 marks) — identifying the relationship between period and frequency in oscillating systems

workedExample.question: A mass of 0.50 kg is attached to a spring and oscillates with simple harmonic motion. The period of oscillation is 2.0 s. Calculate: (a) the frequency of oscillation (2 marks) (b) the spring constant of the spring (3 marks)
steps: Step 1: Calculate frequency using f = 1/T where T = 2.0 s
Step 3: Use the formula T = 2π√(m/k) to find spring constant k
workedExample.markScheme: Marks awarded for: correct formula for frequency (1), correct calculation (1), correct rearrangement of period formula (1), correct substitution of values (1), correct final answer with units (1)

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused amplitude with displacement' — always specify that amplitude is the maximum displacement from equilibrium position
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to convert between period and frequency' — always check if the question asks for period (T) or frequency (f) and remember f = 1/T

Examiner Report 2021: 'Candidates lost marks for not stating energy transformations clearly' — always mention that in SHM, kinetic energy is maximum at equilibrium and potential energy is maximum at maximum displacement
note_data.keyDiagram: Essential diagram: Draw a mass-spring system showing the mass at three positions - maximum displacement left (showing maximum PE, zero KE), equilibrium position (showing zero PE, maximum KE), and maximum displacement right (showing maximum PE, zero KE). Include arrows showing direction of motion and restoring force. Label amplitude (A), equilibrium position, and indicate that restoring force always acts toward equilibrium. This diagram must show energy distribution and force directions clearly.
note_data.quickRecap: Circular motion involves angular displacement measured in radians and angular speed ω = v/r = 2π/T. Objects in circular motion experience centripetal acceleration a = v²/r = ω²r directed toward the center. This requires centripetal force F = mv²/r = mω²r according to Newton's second law. Even at constant speed, circular motion involves changing velocity due to changing direction.
